overview.short: Lakshmi Narasimha Temple is a Hindu temple in Antarvedi, Sakhinetipalle mandal, Konaseema district, Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Lakshmi Narasimha Temple is a Hindu temple in Antarvedi, Sakhinetipalle mandal, Konaseema district, Andhra Pradesh, India. The temple is situated at the place where the Bay of Bengal and Vashista Godavari, a tributary of the Godavari River, meet. It was built in the 15th and 16th centuries.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple, Antarvedi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple, Antarvedi is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple, Antarvedi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajahmundry Airport (87 km) and the nearest railway station is Vijayawada Junction (120 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple, Antarvedi?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple, Antarvedi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple, Antarvedi?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple, Antarvedi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple, Antarvedi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Antarvedi, Antarvedipalem.
